key_findings: v0.1 scope (OwnTracks MQTT subscribe + Actio Placement HTTP + Memoria summary HTTP + Skip rules + Cernere session encryption + audit_log) is fully implemented
IdempotencyCache (src/backend/audit/idempotency.ts) and bearerAuth middleware (src/backend/http/auth-middleware.ts) are dead code — exported but not wired anywhere
Memoria HTTP relay (src/backend/services/memoria-location-http.ts:38-61) has no authentication header; relies on tailnet/loopback assumption that is fragile if MEMORIA_BASE_URL is misconfigured
MQTT broker auth (OWNTRACKS_MQTT_USERNAME/_PASSWORD) is optional by env default; README treats it as recommendation only
Personal-data-non-retention policy strictly upheld: LocationBuffer.flush() drains + discards atomically, audit_log carries only userId/command/requestId
Code hygiene high: 1 file = 1 responsibility, all modules use createChildLogger, spec section numbers referenced in comments, fake-timer flush test included
DB path resolution falls back to process.cwd() (src/backend/server.ts:74-76) — risky for production Electron launch; should use app.getPath('userData')
HTTP relay paths (actio-placement / memoria-location-http) lack unit tests with network mocks

top_issues.title: Memoria HTTP relay has no auth header
Double sort in LocationBuffer skip path

next_actions: Wire bearerAuth or remove (decide at v0.2 kickoff)
Wire IdempotencyCache into coordinator outbound calls
Add per-call service-key to Memoria HTTP relay spec, coordinate with Memoria endpoint PR
Switch Electron dbPath to app.getPath('userData') for production launch
Add HTTP relay unit tests with fetch mock for actio-placement / memoria-location-http
